title: I want to write "READ FIRST" and "TERMS OF PARTICIPATION" on my Monday Form. How do I write texts of 100 words for each of these sections?
excerpt: I want to write “READ FIRST” and “TERMS OF PARTICIPATION” on my Monday Form. How do I write texts of 100 words for each of these sections which are to provide extra details to my audience?

posts.author: Lukman (Lukman Salami)
posts.cookedHtml: I want to write “READ FIRST” and “TERMS OF PARTICIPATION” on my Monday Form. How do I write texts of 100 words for each of these sections which are to provide extra details to my audience?

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Lukman!

Charlotte here from the monday team!

Just to clarify; you are looking to include long text portions for the "READ FIRST"and “TERMS OF PARTICIPATION” parts of your form within the form?

If so, as a workaround to any question title and description word limitations, you can create a monday workdoc with these sections and then download as a PDF and link in the form description.

With this workaround you can create a hyperlink in a form, going one step beyond the clickable URL!

You will have to write in the description in the below format:

<a href=url>text</a>

It will not show up as a hyperlink until you preview it/share it.

Here’s how it will look when editing:

How it will look when shared:

Let me know what you think about this workaround!

posts.author: Lukman (Lukman Salami)
posts.cookedHtml: Yeah, this looks great! Thank you for your help and support, @CharlotteK. This solution solves the issue I have.
